facility_name: China Mobile Hohhot Intelligent Computing Center
cooling_type: Cold plate liquid cooling + indirect evaporative cooling + magnetic levitation AC
opportunity_summary: World's largest single-unit liquid-cooled intelligent computing center operated by a telecom. 6.7 EFLOPS capacity, ~20,000 AI accelerator cards. Cold plate liquid cooling at scale with PUE below 1.15. Opened April 2024. Part of China's Eastern Data Western Computing strategy. Annual electricity savings of 30M kWh across buildings 7 and 9.
notes: PUE reported as both 1.17 and below 1.15 depending on source. 6.7 EFLOPS confirmed. Built with Huawei. 85% domestic AI chips. China's three telcos jointly committed to 50%+ liquid cooling in new projects by 2025 via joint white paper.

facility_name: China Mobile Harbin Intelligent Computing Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (planned, specifics TBD)
opportunity_summary: Planned companion to Hohhot center. Together with Hohhot and Guiyang centers, will deliver computing power equivalent to ~60,000 AI accelerator cards. Severely cold climate (-30C winters) ideal for free cooling supplemented by liquid cooling.
notes: Announced alongside Hohhot opening. Specific liquid cooling details not yet disclosed. Cold climate region should enable very low PUE.

facility_name: China Mobile Guiyang Intelligent Computing Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (planned, specifics TBD)
opportunity_summary: Third node in China Mobile's national AI computing triangle (Hohhot-Harbin-Guiyang). Located in Gui'an New Area data center cluster, a key hub in China's Eastern Data Western Computing strategy. Guizhou is a major DC destination with cool highland climate and cheap hydro power.
notes: Guizhou region already hosts Tencent, Huawei, Apple data centers. Specific liquid cooling plans for this facility not yet publicly detailed.

facility_name: China Telecom Inner Mongolia Information Park
cooling_type: Advanced cooling (liquid cooling adoption under joint telco white paper commitment)
opportunity_summary: 150MW telecom hyperscale campus in Horinger County south of Hohhot. 994,062 sqm site. Part of China's 8 national data center clusters. China Telecom committed via joint white paper (2023) to deploy liquid cooling in 50%+ of new projects by 2025. Exact liquid cooling status at this specific site not confirmed.
notes: 150MW capacity confirmed by Baxtel. China Telecom is one of three telcos that signed the joint liquid cooling commitment. Massive campus but liquid cooling verification needed for this specific site. Construction began 2011, expanded in phases.

facility_name: China Unicom Xining AI Computing Center
cooling_type: Advanced cooling (liquid cooling adoption under joint telco white paper commitment)
opportunity_summary: ~$390M investment. 3,579 PFLOPS currently (target 20,000 PFLOPS). Uses 23,000 domestically made AI chips (Alibaba and others). Located in Qinghai province at high altitude with cold climate. China Unicom committed via joint white paper to 50%+ liquid cooling in new projects by 2025.
notes: Massive domestic AI chip deployment. Specific liquid cooling implementation at this site not confirmed, but China Unicom's joint commitment to liquid cooling applies to new AI facilities.

facility_name: KDDI Oyama Immersion Cooling Data Center
cooling_type: Single-phase immersion cooling (container-type)
opportunity_summary: PUE 1.05 achieved via single-phase immersion cooling in container-type modular data center. 94% reduction in cooling energy vs air cooling. Uses GIGABYTE R282-Z93 and R182-Z91 rack servers. 50kVA IT power, 53.5kVA total. Developed with KDDI, MHI (Mitsubishi Heavy Industries), and NESIC. Commercialization targeted FY2024. Carbon neutrality target by FY2026.
notes: PUE 1.05 is among the lowest reported for any telecom DC worldwide. Container-type design enables rapid edge deployment. KDDI aims for carbon-neutral DCs by FY2026.

facility_name: NTT DATA Keihanna Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (standard equipment, direct-to-chip)
opportunity_summary: 30MW IT load, 10,900 sqm server room. Liquid cooling installed as standard (Green Nexcenter). pPUE 1.15 target. Opening March 2026. NTT also operates a 75kW 'Data Center Trial Field' in Noda City, Chiba for liquid cooling verification (direct liquid cooling + immersion cooling devices). NTT testing two-phase direct-to-chip cooling in existing air-cooled DC.
notes: pPUE 1.15 is partial PUE (facility-level). 30MW confirmed. Liquid cooling as standard is significant for a telecom-group DC. Trial field in Noda City demonstrates both DLC and immersion.

facility_name: NTT DATA Noda Liquid Cooling Trial Field
cooling_type: Direct liquid cooling + immersion cooling (test facility)
opportunity_summary: 105 sqm verification facility opened November 2024 for liquid cooling R&D. Tests both direct liquid cooling and immersion cooling devices. Built by Hibiya Engineering with Kuwana Metals Chilled Tower cooling system. Purpose: verify optimal cooling water temps/flow rates, monitoring systems, staff training. Feeds into NTT DATA's broader liquid cooling deployment.
notes: Small test facility (75kW at opening) but strategically important as NTT DATA's formal liquid cooling R&D center. Opened November 2024.

facility_name: SK Telecom Pangyo AIDC Testbed
cooling_type: Direct liquid cooling + immersion cooling + precision liquid cooling (all three)
opportunity_summary: Korea's first and only facility to deploy all three liquid cooling types simultaneously: DLC, immersion, and precision liquid cooling. Features NVIDIA chips, SK Hynix HBM, GPU virtualization. Opened December 2024. SKT achieved 93% reduction in cooling power vs air cooling in immersion tests. Partnership with Giga Computing and SK Enmove for cooling solutions.
notes: Testbed but operationally significant. SKT planning gigawatt-scale AIDC expansion in Ulsan and other regions. 37% overall power consumption reduction demonstrated.

facility_name: SK Telecom Gasan AI Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (AI-optimized, details not fully disclosed)
opportunity_summary: Launched December 2024 with Lambda partnership. Features NVIDIA H100 GPUs, planned H200 upgrade (March 2025). Part of SKT's 'AI Infrastructure Superhighway' strategy. Liquid cooling systems deployed for AI workloads. SKT offers GPUaaS (GPU-as-a-Service) from this facility.
notes: Liquid cooling confirmed as part of AI-optimized design. Exact cooling type (DLC vs immersion) not specified for this specific site. Part of broader SKT AIDC network.

facility_name: SK Telecom Ulsan AI Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (planned, part of GW-scale build)
opportunity_summary: Planned expansion to 1GW-scale capacity. 69,300 sqm complex described as Korea's most advanced AI-optimized data center featuring liquid cooling systems and AI-driven power management. Part of SKT's three-region AIDC strategy (Seoul metro, southern, southwestern Korea). CEO Jung Jaihun's flagship project.
notes: GW-scale ambition announced at SK AI Summit 2025. Liquid cooling confirmed in facility description. Exact timeline and cooling vendor details TBD.

facility_name: KT-KOGAS LNG-Cooled Data Center
cooling_type: LNG cold energy cooling (waste cold from regasification at -160C)
opportunity_summary: World's first data center cooled by LNG cold energy. Partnership between KT and KOGAS (Korea Gas Corporation). LNG regasification releases cold at -160C. Could save ~12MW of cooling power for a mid-sized DC. 60% reduction in annual power consumption estimated. Located at KOGAS LNG import terminal at Incheon near Seoul.
notes: Novel cooling approach using waste cold energy. Currently in feasibility review and safety verification phase. Not liquid cooling in traditional DC sense but innovative thermal management. If successful, could be replicated at LNG terminals globally.

facility_name: Singtel Nxera DC Tuas
cooling_type: Direct-to-chip liquid cooling (Singapore's largest DLC deployment)
opportunity_summary: 58MW capacity, 120,000 sqft, 8 stories. Singapore's largest direct-to-chip liquid cooling deployment. PUE 1.23 at full load (one of most efficient in Asia). Opened February 2026. Carrier-neutral, multi-tenanted. Nxera total Singapore capacity now 120MW. Co-innovation platform with Deston, SGP Global, QCT, Shell, Stulz, Supermicro for liquid and immersion cooling R&D. NVIDIA GB200 GPU deployment.
notes: PUE reported as 1.23 (design) and 1.25 (some sources). Both verified. 58MW confirmed. Singtel expanding liquid-cooled DCs to Indonesia and Thailand via Nxera.

facility_name: Telefonica Bellas Vistas Immersion Cooling
cooling_type: Single-phase immersion cooling (Submer SmartPod)
opportunity_summary: Submer SmartPod immersion cooling deployed at Bellas Vistas central office. 50kW thermal capacity, TIER III reliability. Servers submerged in non-conductive, non-toxic, biodegradable liquid. Up to 50% more energy efficient than air cooling. Coolant stable below 55C even under max load. Deployed June 2023. Supports edge computing and 5G growth. Telefonica serves 365M+ customers across Europe and LATAM.
notes: Deployed in a telco central office, not a greenfield DC -- significant as a model for retrofitting existing telco infrastructure. Submer is a Spanish company. Telefonica has hundreds of edge locations that could adopt this.

facility_name: Orange Padus Lab
cooling_type: Immersion cooling + liquid cooling (Submer + Vertiv) + rear-door heat exchangers
opportunity_summary: Prefab R&D data center in Lannion (Brittany telecom hub). Tests immersion cooling (servers in oil bath), liquid cooling (targeted component cooling), Vertiv Liebert DCD rear-door heat exchangers, 400V HVDC power, green hydrogen. Built by Vertiv in Zagreb, shipped to Lannion. Partners: Submer, Dell, AVELTEC, EnerSys. Model for Orange edge/5G deployment globally. 'Padus' means sustainable in Breton.
notes: R&D facility, not production scale -- but Orange VP Gabriel Sidhom stated immersion cooling is strategic for edge/urban DCs. Orange has >365M customers globally. Lab results will drive fleet-wide adoption.

facility_name: BT Adastral Park Liquid Cooling Trials
cooling_type: Immersion cooling (Immersion4) + cold plate (Nexalus) + spray immersion (Airsys)
opportunity_summary: BT trialing three liquid cooling approaches at Adastral Park research center: (1) Full immersion via Immersion4 (Switzerland) -- 70% energy savings, (2) Cold plate via Nexalus, (3) Spray partial immersion via Airsys. Each saves 40-50% cooling power vs air. Demonstrated at BT Sustainability Festival Sept 2023. BT plans to deploy different solutions across its estate based on location/needs.
notes: Trial phase, not production deployment yet. BT also partnered with Iceotope for Juniper network switch liquid cooling. BT's DC estate spans UK and globally. Also partnered with Johnson Controls for cooling optimization.

facility_name: Deutsche Telekom T-Systems Magdeburg DC (Water-Cooled Rack Pilot)
cooling_type: Water-cooled racks (pilot) + conventional air cooling
opportunity_summary: Pilot project with water-cooled racks comparing efficiency vs conventional air cooling. Part of T-Systems/Fraunhofer IFF net-zero sustainability partnership. PUE 1.3 at nearby Biere DC (PUE 1.28). Goal: net-zero off-grid data center. Analyzed 98 technologies. Potential to save 20,000 tons CO2/year. DT uses 100% renewable energy since 2021.
notes: Water-cooled rack pilot is early stage for DT. PUE 1.3/1.28 at Biere/Magdeburg -- room for improvement with liquid cooling. DT is Europe's largest telco by revenue.

facility_name: Telenor AI Factory
cooling_type: Advanced cooling (renewable energy powered, waste heat for district heating)
opportunity_summary: Norway's first sovereign AI factory. Opened November 2024. NVIDIA H100 DGX systems on Supermicro infrastructure. 100% renewable energy. Surplus heat repurposed for district heating. Planning new dedicated AI data center in Oslo. Red Hat partnership for sovereign cloud (announced MWC 2026). Customers include Hive Autonomy, Capgemini.
notes: Liquid cooling specifics not publicly disclosed, but DGX H100 systems typically require liquid cooling at scale. Planning larger Oslo DC with waste heat reuse. Nordic climate enables very low PUE.

facility_name: Reliance Jio Edge Data Centers (Immersion Cooling)
cooling_type: Single-phase immersion cooling (Asperitas AIC24, natural convection)
opportunity_summary: Jio deploying Asperitas AIC24 immersion cooling for mobile edge compute. Natural convection (no pumps). Each module: 22kW IT in <10 sqft. Uses Supermicro/AMD/Micron hardware. Designed for cell tower edge deployments. Jio network handles 3B GB/month, growing to 10B GB. Separate GW-scale Jamnagar campus (120MW Phase 1 by late 2026) with NVIDIA partnership.
notes: Edge immersion cooling deployment at scale is unique for a telecom. AIC24 uses natural convection -- zero mechanical pumps. Jamnagar campus cooling details not yet disclosed but will likely include liquid cooling given NVIDIA GPU density.

facility_name: Reliance Jio Jamnagar AI Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (expected for NVIDIA GPU density, specifics TBD)
opportunity_summary: Massive 1GW campus target (3GW ultimate). Phase 1: 120MW by late 2026. NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchip and DGX Cloud. Powered by renewable energy. India's first GPU-as-a-Service cloud. $110B overall investment program. Liquid cooling highly likely given GPU density but not formally confirmed.
notes: MW figures from reports, not official press release. 120MW Phase 1 is approximate. Cooling details will likely emerge as construction progresses. Massive telecom AI play.

facility_name: du-Microsoft Abu Dhabi Hyperscale Data Center
cooling_type: Immersion-ready cooling infrastructure
opportunity_summary: USD $544.5M (AED 2B) hyperscale data center. du builds and operates, Microsoft as anchor tenant. Immersion-ready cooling infrastructure. Announced April 2025 at Dubai AI Week. Turner & Townsend appointed for project management (Aug 2025). Capacity delivered in phases. First major telecom-hyperscaler immersion-ready DC in Middle East.
notes: Immersion-ready confirmed -- designed to support immersion cooling even if initial deployment uses other methods. $544M is a significant telecom DC investment. Desert climate makes advanced cooling critical.

facility_name: Vodafone Data Centers (HVAC Efficiency Program)
cooling_type: Natural fresh air cooling + HVAC coating optimization (36 urban DCs upgraded)
opportunity_summary: Vodafone DCs achieve PUE 1.2 or lower. Switching from mechanical to natural fresh air cooling systems. Large DCs in Ireland, Italy, Germany, UK. Investing over 10 years in DC migration and efficiency. 93% reduction in operational emissions since FY2020. CDP 'A List' for 2024. No confirmed liquid/immersion cooling deployment yet -- potential future adopter.
notes: NOTE: The HVAC coating story (Facilities Dive) is actually about Verizon, not Vodafone -- source was returned in Vodafone search results. Vodafone PUE 1.2 is from Data Centre Magazine. Vodafone has not publicly announced liquid cooling deployment.

facility_name: Verizon Urban Data Centers (HVAC Upgrade Program)
cooling_type: HVAC coating optimization ($30M nationwide upgrade planned)
opportunity_summary: Ener.co HVAC coating deployed across 36 urban DCs in NE US. $1.28M annual energy savings, 1,700 metric ton GHG reduction/year. 30-month payback. $16M projected savings over next decade. $30M nationwide expansion planned. No liquid/immersion cooling announced yet -- major untapped opportunity for world's largest telecom infrastructure.
notes: Verizon is investing in cooling efficiency but via HVAC optimization, not liquid cooling. No public liquid cooling or immersion announcements found. Given Verizon's massive DC estate, this represents a large future opportunity.

facility_name: Globe/STT Fairview Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid-cooled capable (designed to house liquid-cooled servers)
opportunity_summary: 124MW total capacity, Philippines' largest data center. Phase 1 opened Q2 2025. Designed to house liquid-cooled solutions and servers. Joint venture with STT GDC (Singapore). Tropical climate makes efficient cooling critical. Liquid immersion cooling supports AI/HPC workloads and reduces energy/water consumption in the tropics.
notes: 124MW confirmed. Liquid-cooled capable confirmed by Globe but initial deployment details not specified. STT GDC is Singtel-linked, so Nxera cooling expertise likely transferable.

facility_name: Indosat BDx CGK4 AI Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (AI-ready, powered by renewable hydropower)
opportunity_summary: Indonesia's first sovereign AI data center. Launched December 2024 with NVIDIA accelerated computing. BDx Indonesia JV (Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison + Lintasarta + BDx Data Centers). Powered by green hydropower. 15MW facility near Jakarta, 100MW hyperscale campus planned at Suryacipta. Southeast Asia's most interconnected AI infrastructure.
notes: Liquid cooling confirmed as AI-ready. 15MW is initial phase. 100MW hyperscale campus planned. Indosat is Indonesia's second-largest telco.

facility_name: Telstra InfraCo Minchinbury AI Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid cooling and AI-native rack density support (designed in)
opportunity_summary: 62MW AI-optimized data center in Western Sydney. JV with Starwood Capital Group and Doma Infrastructure Group. Liquid cooling and AI-native rack densities built into design. Carrier-neutral. Development approval secured. Construction start early 2026, RFS early Q1 2028. Telstra InfraCo also offers liquid DLC and immersion capabilities across existing portfolio.
notes: 62MW confirmed. Liquid cooling support confirmed in design. Telstra InfraCo already advertises liquid DLC and immersion capabilities. Australia's largest telco entering hyperscale AI DC market.

facility_name: Swisscom Swiss AI Platform Data Center
cooling_type: Advanced cooling (NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, liquid cooling implied)
opportunity_summary: NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD-based Swiss AI Platform launched November 2024. GPU-as-a-Service for Swiss enterprises. Tier IV-certified Bern-Wankdorf hall. Sovereign AI infrastructure. DGX SuperPOD systems typically require liquid cooling at scale. Swisscom + NVIDIA partnership for sovereign AI widens domestic compute pool.
notes: Liquid cooling not explicitly confirmed in sources but DGX SuperPOD architecture strongly implies it. Swisscom is Switzerland's dominant telco. Strategic for Swiss data sovereignty requirements.

facility_name: Fastweb NeXXt AI Factory
cooling_type: Advanced cooling (NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, liquid cooling implied)
opportunity_summary: Italy's first NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD. 31 DGX H100 systems. Launched July 2024. Powers MIIA model (1.5B tokens, largest Italian-language LLM). Most powerful SuperPOD owned by a private Italian company. DGX SuperPOD architecture strongly implies liquid cooling. Fastweb is Swisscom subsidiary, Italian telco.
notes: Liquid cooling not explicitly confirmed in available sources but DGX H100 SuperPOD at this scale almost certainly uses liquid cooling. Bergamo location confirmed.

facility_name: Telia Helsinki Data Center (HDC)
cooling_type: District heating waste heat recovery (90% capacity by spring 2025)
opportunity_summary: Telia Helsinki DC expanding by 4MW (completion early 2025). Heat recovery capacity increasing to 90% (from 60%). Waste heat heats 28,000+ Helsinki apartments. AI driving 30% annual capacity growth in Nordics. Telia operates 13 DCs across 9 regions. No immersion/liquid cooling confirmed yet -- primarily heat recovery leader.
notes: Not a liquid cooling deployer yet but highly relevant as waste heat recovery leader. Could adopt liquid cooling to enhance heat capture quality. Nordic telecoms are sustainability frontrunners.

facility_name: STC center3 Data Centers
cooling_type: Green cooling initiatives (immersion cooling mandated by SASO regulation for new builds)
opportunity_summary: STC and subsidiary center3 anchor Saudi Arabia's DC market. Expanding capacity with AI-ready designs. SASO (Saudi Standards Organization) 2023 regulation mandates advanced cooling (including liquid immersion) for all new DC facilities. STC collaborating with Humain on new DCs. Vision 2030 driving green DC investment. KSA immersion cooling market valued at $15M and growing.
notes: STC has not publicly confirmed specific immersion cooling deployments, but Saudi regulation now mandates it for new builds. center3 is STC's DC subsidiary. Desert climate makes efficient cooling essential.

facility_name: MTN Nigeria Sifiso Dabengwa Data Centre
cooling_type: Advanced cooling (AI-grade, specifics not disclosed)
opportunity_summary: $240M investment. 9MW total (Phase 1: 4.5MW Tier III commissioned July 2025). West Africa's largest Tier III data center. AI-driven sovereign cloud (Genova program). Compliant with Nigeria Data Protection Act. No specific liquid cooling confirmed, but facility designed for HPC/AI workloads.
notes: MTN has not confirmed liquid/immersion cooling at this facility. Africa DC immersion cooling market projected at $50M by 2025, $81M by 2030. MTN planning AI-grade DCs across Africa -- potential early adopter on continent.

facility_name: China Telecom / HiCloud Underwater Data Center (Shanghai Lingang)
cooling_type: Seawater immersion cooling (underwater pod)
opportunity_summary: 24MW underwater data center pod off Shanghai coast near Lingang area. Powered by offshore wind farm. 90% less cooling power vs conventional DCs. China Telecom exploring 'land + subsea' hybrid cloud deployment and marine 5G enhancement. HiCloud plans 500MW UDC expansion.
notes: Operated by HiCloud Technology with China Telecom backing. Seawater acts as natural immersion coolant. First commercial UDC in China. China Telecom considering offering hybrid land+subsea cloud to customers.

facility_name: China Unicom Wudang Cloud Valley Data Center (Shiyan)
cooling_type: Natural water cooling (Danjiangkou Reservoir water)
opportunity_summary: First big data center in Hubei Province using natural water cooling instead of electricity-powered air conditioning. Located in Danjiangkou City near the Danjiangkou Reservoir, leveraging abundant cold water supply for sustainable cooling.
notes: China Unicom Shiyan Branch project. Water-cooled rather than immersion or cold plate -- uses reservoir water piped directly for heat exchange. Novel approach leveraging local hydrology.

facility_name: SK Telecom Incheon HQ Immersion Cooling (GRC)
cooling_type: Full immersion cooling (GRC + SK Enmove dielectric fluid)
opportunity_summary: Immersion cooling verified at SKT Incheon HQ with Green Revolution Cooling (GRC, Texas). 4-month test from June 2023: 93% cooling power reduction, 37% total power reduction, 10%+ server power savings vs air cooling. Full immersion deployment at dedicated AI DC at Incheon HQ.
notes: Announced November 2023. Partners: GRC (immersion tanks), SK Enmove (thermal fluids), various server manufacturers. Distinct from Pangyo testbed -- this is production immersion cooling at HQ facility.

facility_name: NTT Yokohama 1 Data Center (Green Nexcenter)
cooling_type: Liquid cooling system (standard, direct liquid cooling)
opportunity_summary: Japan's first ultra energy-efficient DC service compatible with liquid cooling technology (Green Nexcenter). pPUE 1.15 achieved. Liquid cooling as standard infrastructure. Opened fiscal 2024. Serves Tokyo metropolitan area.
notes: Part of NTT's Green Nexcenter product line. Liquid cooling system standard (not optional). Distinct from Keihanna (air-cooled + DLC-ready) and Noda (test field).

facility_name: KDDI Osaka Sakai AI Data Center
cooling_type: Hybrid: air cooling + direct liquid cooling (NVIDIA GB200 NVL72)
opportunity_summary: 48MW AI data center at former Sharp Sakai Plant. 57,000 sqm, 4-story facility. Direct liquid cooling for NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 Blackwell+Grace systems. 100% renewable energy. KDDI GPU Cloud service launched Jan 22, 2026. Partnership with HPE.
notes: KDDI acquired Sharp Sakai Plant April 2025. Reuses existing high-capacity power and cooling infrastructure. Distinct from KDDI Oyama immersion facility. Operations commenced January 22, 2026.

facility_name: SoftBank Sakai AI Data Center (former Sharp LCD Plant)
cooling_type: Direct-to-chip liquid cooling + air cooling
opportunity_summary: Massive AI DC at former Sharp LCD factory. 150MW initial, expandable to 400MW. 440,000 sqm site, 750,000 sqm total floor. Joint ¥100B ($676M) investment with OpenAI. Direct-to-chip liquid cooling for 1,224+ NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs (Dec 2025 launch), scaling to 4,000+ GPUs / 10.6 ExaFLOPS. METI certified. Powers Sarashina LLM.
notes: Construction started autumn 2024, full operations 2025. SoftBank acquired ~60% of Sharp Sakai Plant land, reusing buildings and power/cooling infrastructure. Separate site from KDDI's Sakai facility (different parts of the plant complex).

facility_name: SoftBank Tomakomai AI Data Center (Hokkaido)
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (expected, not confirmed; cool climate + renewable energy focus)
opportunity_summary: Next-gen AI DC in Hokkaido. 60-70% complete as of Feb 2026. Opens FY2026 (by March 2027). METI subsidy-funded. Cool climate ideal for efficient cooling. Renewable energy potential. Decentralized from Tokyo/Osaka for disaster resilience.
notes: Specific cooling technology not confirmed in press materials but SoftBank's strategy centers on liquid-cooled AI infrastructure (per Sakai). The Tech Capital reports 250MW target. Container-type UPS and emergency generators for rapid construction.

facility_name: Singtel Nxera / Telekom Malaysia Johor Data Center Campus
cooling_type: Liquid cooling (LEED-certified, high-density AI/GPU support)
opportunity_summary: Joint venture DC campus in Iskandar Puteri, Johor, near Singapore-Malaysia subsea cable links. 64MW initial phase, scalable to 200MW. LEED certified with liquid cooling for high-density AI and GPU workloads. Targets hyperscalers and GPU-as-a-Service providers.
notes: Partnership between Singtel's Nxera and Telekom Malaysia (TM). Two telecom operators collaborating on liquid-cooled AI-ready infrastructure. Strategic location near subsea cables connecting Singapore and Malaysia.
